Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund, and submit the same to the COA,
duly certified by the Municipal Accountant, pursuant to the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to:
a. strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the
timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report to
the COA;
b. comply with the submission of the GPB and GAD AR to the Provincial Planning
and Development Office and the DILG Provincial Office for their review,
endorsement and consolidation as provided under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC
Nos. 2016-01 and 2013-01; and
c. submit immediately the Utilization Report for CY 2019 to the Office of the Auditor
for evaluation of the expenses incurred.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMB and the MENRO to
review and update the Ten Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality
every two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 4 (g), Rule VI of the
IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize the MSWMB and conform
with the membership requirements in accordance with the Rule VI of the IRR of RA
No. 9003.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) and/or the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare and submit the RPCPPE for Road Networks, Floor Control Systems, Water Supply Systems, Parks, Plazas and Monuments, Other Infrastructure Assets, Other Structures, PPE and Medical Equipment and Motor Vehicles;
b. Identify unserviceable property, prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), and dispose the said property in accordance with existing rules and regulations; and
c. Analyze the difference between the RPCPPE and the accounting records, afterwhich make the necessary adjustment.
observations.managementAction:
Work back on the details of the inventory report and accounting records to determine the discrepancy of Accounting Report of RPCPPE.
Updating of records for the unserviceable properties is on-going. Partial list of unserviceable property were submitted to the Audit Team in July, 2020.
The Municipal GSO has reduced the continued discrepancy reconciling their records for necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: The Management was unable to complete the inventory of unserviceable property due to lack of time and man-power.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MSWMB to review and update the Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality every two years or as the need arises in compliance with Section 12 of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Management is currently reviewing the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan. They are assessing the existing condition of the Municipality’s waste management system and possible solutions for updating/ incorporation in the Plan.
observations.reason: Review of the existing Solid Waste Management Plan is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require execute Contract of Lease between the Municipal Government of Pagsanjan and the 185 stall holders in compliance with Section 23 of Pagsanjan Market Code of 2004 for additional income of the Local Government Unit (LGU).
observations.managementAction: The new Market Administrator prioritized the preparation of the 1st batch of contract of lease.
observations.reason: The lease was overlooked due to change of administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate the required one percent of the IRA for the programs, projects and activities of the Local Council for the Protection of Children in strict compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The Management allocated 2.81% or P3,010,902.88 from its Internal Revenue Allotment of P107,128,120.00 for CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat and the General Services Officer (GSO) to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts and purchase orders, together with the supporting documents, within five working days from its transactions perfection/issuance, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate review of compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations, completeness of documentation and evaluation whether contractual covenant are not disadvantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: Contracts for infrastructure projects and Purchase Orders for procurement of goods in CY 2018 and the 1st semester of CY 2019 were already submitted to the previous Audit Team in CY 2019. While the remaining transactions for CY 2019, were submitted on August 2020.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Government utilize fully the 20 per cent Development Fund for purposes provided under DILG-DBM JMC No. 2011-1 to ensure that the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes are fully met.
observations.managementAction: The Management strictly monitors implementation of development projects and registered a utilization rate of 81.69%. The remaining projects were on-going as of year-end and were completed in the 1st quarter of CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor maximize the utilization of the 70 per cent allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery to effectively manage and reduce the impact of disaster.
observations.managementAction: The Management strictly monitors the implementation of DRRM activities. As per records, PPAs for LDRRMF in CY 2019 were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to see to it that all GAD projects are supportive of gender issues to promote gender-responsive governance, protect human rights and promote women’s economic empowerment.
observations.managementAction: GAD PPAs for CY 2019 were approved by the Provincial DILG. These were all relevant to gender issues and were implemented in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate the required amount of 1% of the IRA for the programs, projects and activities of the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children in strict compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality still failed to allocate 1% of IRA for LCPC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to furnish the Audit Team with copies of purchase orders within five working days from its issuance, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate the review of compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations and completeness of documentation.
observations.managementAction: Copy of purchase orders were submitted but days or even months beyond the required 5 working days from issuance of PO.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct
the General Services Officer to:
• prepare and submit RPCPPE for Road Networks, Flood Control Systems,
Water Supply Systems, Parks, Plazas & Monuments, Other Infrastructure
Assets, Construction and Heavy Equipment and Disaster Response and
Rescue Equipment;
• identify unserviceable property, prepare the Inspection and Inventory
Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), and dispose the said property in
accordance with existing rules and regulations;
• maintain property cards for property, plant and equipment and work
animals to account for the receipt and disposition of the same; and
• the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to analyze the
difference between their records, afterwhich make the necessary adjustment.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Budget Officer to allocate the required amount of 1% of the IRA for
the programs, projects and activities of the Municipal Council for the
Protection of Children in strict compliance with Section 15 of RA No. 9344.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts within five (5)
working days from its execution, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001,
to facilitate review of compliance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations,
completeness of documentation and evaluation whether contractual covenants
are not disadvantageous to the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instructs the Municipal
Budget Officer to prepare and submit the Statement of Appropriations,
Allotments, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) for current and continuing
appropriations to facilitate the monitoring of expenditures and balances of the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require
the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to furnish the Audit Team with copies of
purchase orders within five working days from its issuance, as required under
COA Circular No. 2009-001, to facilitate the review of compliance with
pertinent laws, rules and regulations and completeness of documentation.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the details of the discrepancies and to prepare a quarterly reconciliation statement of Cash in Bank’s General Ledger and cashbook balances and make the necessary adjustments/corrections on the discrepancies noted; and the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS in the prescribed format for all depository accounts and to submit to the Office of the Auditor for verification within ten days after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine the details of the discrepancies and to prepare a quarterly reconciliation statement of Cash in Bank’s General Ledger and cashbook balances and make the necessary adjustments/corrections on the discrepancies noted; and the Municipal Accountant to prepare BRS in the prescribed format for all depository accounts and to submit to the Office of the Auditor for verification within ten days after the end of each month. The Municipal Accountant reconciled their records and determined the discrepancies leaving only P13,089.74 as unreconciled amount as of December 31, 2017. The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer (GSO) to: • prepare and submit RPCPPE for Road Networks, Water Supply Systems, Power Supply Systems, Parks, Plazas and Monuments, Other Infrastructure Assets and Hospital and Health Centers; • identify unserviceable property, prepare the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), and dispose the said property in accordance with existing rules and regulations; and • analyze the difference between their accounting records, afterwhich the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Only RPCPPE for Road Supply Systems, Water Supply Systems, Power Supply Systems, Parks, Plazas and Monuments, Other Infrastructure Assets and Hospital and Health Centers was submitted in CY 2017. No IIRUP was submitted in CY 2017. The difference between the accounting and treasury records were not yet reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to address the difference between the accounts RPT Receivable and SET Receivable and make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the JEV taking into account the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made and a JEV was issued to adjust the difference.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government utilize fully the 20% Development Fund for purposes provided under Section 287 of RA No. 7160 to ensure that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes are met.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of 20% Development Fund increased to 53.31% in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to maximize the utilization of the 70% allocation for disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery in order to anticipate, respond to and recover from the impacts of likely, imminent and current hazard events or conditions.
observations.managementAction: Utilization of 70% DRRM fund increased from 40.62% to 95.64%. Also, included in the fund is Food Supplies Expenses of DRRMO personnel in times of calamity amounting to P100,000.00 which was not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the Municipal Treasurer that the grant of discounts on prompt payment of RPT and SET Receivables is applicable only once from January 1 to March 31 of each year except when payment of taxes was made on installment basis. However, in installment, once the taxpayer failed to pay on time for the first quarter, interest will be charged to the whole amount of tax due and discounts for the next three quarters will not be applicable even if payments were made on time. Also, interest on late payment shall start from January, thus, late payment for the month of April should be charged with interest at 2% per month multiply by the number of month in arrears or by 4 months, that is January to April in this case.
observations.managementAction: The service provider and revision was coordinated and the system revision was made on the application of discounts and penalties. The revised application of discounts and penalties was implemented in CY 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to address the difference between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and make the necessary adjustments, furnishing the Office of the Auditor with a copy of the JEV taking into account the adjustments made.
observations.managementAction: A JEV was made to adjust the difference which is due to transfer of the 30% quick response balance which was receipted under Trust Fund only on January 9, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit all municipal accounts for the month of December 2017 within the prescribed period to the Audit Team to facilitate timely audit. Also, we recommended that the quantity of DVs and ORs submitted be included in the transmittal letter.
observations.managementAction: Municipal accounts for the month of December 2017 were submitted as of March 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned officials coordinate with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) Sta. Cruz Branch as an initial step to the shift of the Municipality from manual to automated payroll system to address the risk of loss and to ensure the timely delivery of salaries to all employees of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: An ATM machine was already installed and functioning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point to submit to the DBM two copies of the annual GAD accomplishment report for the previous year along with the agency budget proposal in accordance with the budget call.
observations.managementAction: GAD accomplishme nt report for the previous year along with the agency budget proposal was submitted to the DBM.
